what_it_does: Records a raised signal notice that the global visual-fidelity decision has been decided with the option 'Push the drawn style further first', and that work gated on that decision can resume.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id, board:// address, type=signal, source=claude_code, state=raised, author_session=operator
A links entry of kind attached_to points to decision://global/visual-fidelity
History array records a single state transition from null to raised by operator
Body text names the decision, the chosen option, and states that gated work may RESUME
standouts: It is a decision.decided signal type noticeboard item
Links explicitly to decision://global/visual-fidelity via attached_to
The resume itself is described as the lane's/operator's gated step
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter metadata for ledger items
Uses board:// and decision:// URI schemes
Includes a history array for state transitions
notes: A noticeboard signal that unblocks visual-fidelity work by announcing the chosen 'drawn style' option.
questions: Which specific work items or lanes are gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity?
Who performs the gated resume step and how is it tracked?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to announce the decided visual-fidelity option and unblock gated work; the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: To communicate the resolution of a design decision so downstream code/design work can proceed with the selected visual direction.
contribution: Provides a durable, timestamped signal that records the decision outcome and its gating effect, enabling downstream processes to resume with the chosen visual-fidelity option.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard signal item recording that the global visual-fidelity decision chose 'Push the drawn style further first' and unblocks gated work.
intention_for_embedding: Notify the system that the visual-fidelity decision is resolved and downstream lanes may resume using the selected drawn-style option.
